column.name: BeatId
column.description: Identifier for the assigned sales beat/territory for the visit or order line, typically a numeric code stored as text. May be blank/null when the order is not mapped to a beat.

column.name: BeatName
column.description: Human-readable name of the sales beat/market area (e.g., locality or route name). Use for reporting by beat; may be empty when BeatId is null.

column.name: BeatRoute
column.description: Optional route descriptor for the beat (e.g., route name/sequence) used in field planning. Often blank in this extract, so treat as informational only.

column.name: BeatSchedule
column.description: Frequency or schedule code for the beat (e.g., 'Daily' or '0' when unknown/not maintained). Analysts should not assume '0' means no schedule; treat as missing/unspecified.

column.name: BeatType
column.description: Planned visit cadence for the beat such as Daily/Weekly/Fortnight/Monthly. Useful for coverage planning and comparing expected vs actual activity.

column.name: BillNo
column.description: Billing prefix/type or bill series indicator (e.g., 'RR', 'PR') associated with the order/invoice. May be blank for orders not yet billed or where billing reference is not captured.

column.name: ClientCity
column.description: City of the customer/outlet placing the order. Use for geographic segmentation; values reflect master data and may differ from distributor city.

column.name: ClientCreationDate
column.description: Date the client/outlet record was created in the system. Useful for cohorting new outlet additions and analyzing early ordering behavior.

column.name: ClientRefrence
column.description: External or legacy reference for the client (often null/blank). Treat 'null' string values as missing when cleaning.

column.name: ClientState
column.description: State/territory of the client as stored in master data (may include internal state codes like 'MP-JBP'). Use for regional rollups but validate coding conventions.

column.name: ClientZone
column.description: Sales zone grouping for the client (e.g., West, South, North, East-1). Use for high-level regional performance reporting.

column.name: Client_Id
column.description: Unique identifier of the client/outlet in the system (indexed). Always filter/join on this for outlet-level analysis and to avoid full-table scans.

column.name: Client_Name
column.description: Customer/outlet name as entered in master data. Primarily for display and validation; not a stable key due to spelling variations.

column.name: Client_Type
column.description: Business classification of the client such as Retailer, Parlor, Display Retailer. Use to segment sales by channel/outlet type.

column.name: ContactType
column.description: Sub-classification of the outlet (e.g., Chemist, Cosmetic, Unisex, General Merchant). Useful for finer channel analysis; values are free-text and may vary.

column.name: Designation
column.description: Field sales role/designation associated with the order (e.g., TSO, Sr. TSI). Use to analyze performance by role level; not necessarily the employee’s HR title system-wide.

column.name: DistributerCode
column.description: Code for the supplying distributor, if maintained. Often blank/null; prefer distributor name or a separate distributor master key if available.

column.name: DistributerDivision
column.description: Distributor business division/portfolio tag (e.g., Retail, Parlor,Both,Hair). Use cautiously as it can be multi-valued/free-text.

column.name: DistributerName
column.description: Name of the distributor fulfilling the order. Use for distributor-level reporting; not guaranteed unique/stable as a key.

column.name: DistributerSubType
column.description: Additional distributor classification (often blank). Treat as optional metadata.

column.name: DistributerType
column.description: High-level partner type for the distributor record (commonly 'Distributor'). Helpful when the dataset includes multiple partner types.

column.name: Division
column.description: Commercial division/channel for the transaction (e.g., Retail, Parlor). Use for primary business line reporting.

column.name: DstrCity
column.description: City of the distributor supplying the order. Useful for supply network and territory mapping; may differ from client city.

column.name: DstrState
column.description: State of the distributor (may include internal codes). Use for distributor footprint reporting.

column.name: DstrZone
column.description: Zone of the distributor (e.g., West, South). Useful to analyze sales by distributor geography.

column.name: EmpCity
column.description: City associated with the selling employee/field rep. Use for workforce/territory analysis; may reflect HQ or posting rather than client location.

column.name: EmpName
column.description: Name of the employee/field rep linked to the order. Use for attribution in dashboards but avoid as a join key due to name changes/duplicates.

column.name: EmpState
column.description: State associated with the employee’s posting. Useful for staffing and coverage reporting.

column.name: EmpZone
column.description: Zone associated with the employee’s posting. Use for regional salesforce analysis and hierarchy rollups.

column.name: Function
column.description: Optional functional tag for the employee or transaction (often null/blank). Treat as non-mandatory descriptive metadata.

column.name: GST
column.description: GST rate/percentage applicable (e.g., '18.00') stored as text. Interpret as a tax rate, not an amount; clean 'null' strings as missing.

column.name: HQState
column.description: State of the employee’s/headquarter or assigned HQ. Useful for organizational alignment and HQ-based reporting.

column.name: Label
column.description: Order label/reference used for tracking (e.g., internal label, pack label, or system reference like 'QS…'). Not guaranteed unique; use alongside Order_Id if needed.

column.name: MRP
column.description: Maximum Retail Price for the product/variant at the time of extraction, often per unit. Can be 0 or null when not maintained; do not use as selling price.

column.name: OrderDateTime
column.description: Timestamp when the order was created/recorded. Use for intraday analysis and sequencing; Order_Date is the date-only version for partitioning/filtering.

column.name: OrderValueWODiscount
column.description: Total order line value before discounts (typically base value) in decimal. Use to compute discount impact when compared with net amounts; confirm whether it is line-level or order-level in your query context.

column.name: Order_Amt
column.description: Net order amount (often including taxes/after discounts) represented as float. Use for revenue reporting but prefer decimal fields where possible to avoid rounding issues.

column.name: Order_Date
column.description: Order date (date-only, indexed) for filtering large queries and building daily/weekly/monthly aggregations. Use as the primary date for most time-series reporting unless time-of-day is required.

column.name: Order_Id
column.description: Unique order identifier (indexed) representing the parent order for the SKU line. Use to group multiple SKU rows into a single order and to join to order header datasets.

column.name: PDDescription
column.description: Product/division description text (often category/segment narrative) that may include concatenated attributes. Treat as descriptive and potentially messy/free-text.

column.name: PositionCode
column.description: Sales position/territory code (e.g., 'NER_DEL_FF8') representing the rep’s assignment. Useful for hierarchy mapping; may be null for some records.

column.name: ProDivision
column.description: Product or commercial division tag for the item (often aligns with Division such as Retail/Both). Use to analyze product mix across divisions; verify consistency with Division.

column.name: ProductCategory
column.description: Product category or SKU grouping label (e.g., 'Gold Bleach 43g', 'Hair Remover'). Use for category-level sales analysis; values may be more granular than Product_Category.

column.name: ProductCode
column.description: Product/SKU code used in ordering (e.g., alphanumeric like 'RR50039' or numeric ERP codes). Useful for operational mapping; may match VSKUCode in many cases.

column.name: ProductCreationDate
column.description: Date/time when the product master record was created, stored as text. Parse carefully if needed; use mainly for product lifecycle/cohort analysis.

column.name: ProductPrice
column.description: Reference price for the product (e.g., list/base price) as float. Not necessarily the transacted unit price; compare with Unit_Price for effective selling price.

column.name: Product_Category
column.description: Alternate product category field (often similar to ProductCategory). Use one consistently in reporting after checking which has better completeness/standardization.

column.name: Product_Id
column.description: Unique product identifier (indexed) for joining to product master and for stable product-level analysis. Prefer over Product_Name or ProductCode when available.

column.name: Product_Name
column.description: Human-readable product name/description. Useful for display; not a stable key and may change over time.

column.name: Quantity
column.description: Number of units ordered for this SKU line. Multiply by Unit_Price to approximate line value (subject to discounts/taxes depending on definitions).

column.name: SSId
column.description: Identifier likely representing salesperson/stockist/secondary sales mapping or a system-specific entity tied to the transaction. Use as an internal key for operational joins when documented; otherwise treat as a linking ID.

column.name: ServiceTaxApplicableOn
column.description: Identifier indicating the entity/region/tax rule record used to apply tax. Not a monetary value; use mainly for tax-rule audits and joins to tax configuration tables.

column.name: TLCode
column.description: Team lead/manager code for the supervising role above the employee. Use to roll up sales under manager hierarchies; typically paired with TLName.

column.name: TLDesignation
column.description: Designation of the team lead (e.g., ASM, TSM, BDM). Useful for management-level performance slices.

column.name: TLName
column.description: Name of the team lead/manager associated with the employee/order. Use for display; do not rely on as a unique identifier.

column.name: TodayBeatCode
column.description: Code for the beat planned/visited on that day (often blank in samples). Use for daily route/beat adherence analysis when populated.

column.name: TodayBeatId
column.description: Beat identifier for the specific day’s plan/visit tied to the order (may equal BeatId). Use to analyze day-level beat assignment even if the master BeatId is missing.

column.name: TodayBeatName
column.description: Name of the day’s beat plan/visit tied to the order. Useful for operational reporting; may be 'null'/blank when TodayBeatId is null.

column.name: TotalDiscountAmount
column.description: Total discount amount applied (decimal), typically at line level in this extract but may represent allocated discount. Use with OrderValueWODiscount/Unit_Price to compute net pricing; validate allocation logic for multi-line orders.

column.name: Unit_Price
column.description: Effective unit selling price used to value the line (often equals OrderValueWODiscount divided by Quantity). Use as the primary metric for realized price per unit, excluding/including discounts based on business definition.

column.name: VDDescription
column.description: Variant/division description text similar to PDDescription, sometimes repeating extended attributes. Treat as descriptive/free-text and not suitable as a key.

column.name: VSKUCode
column.description: Variant/SKU code for the sellable unit; often matches ProductCode but can differ when variants exist. Use for SKU-level analysis when ProductCode is ambiguous.

column.name: Variant_Id
column.description: Unique identifier for the product variant/SKU (sellable unit). Use for the most granular product analysis and joins to variant master data.

column.name: Variant_Name
column.description: Human-readable name of the variant/SKU (sometimes includes status like '(Active)' or may hold an alternate code). Use for display; not a stable key.
